role_summary: VP Marketing at Alloy Partners (formerly High Alpha Innovation). 9+ years building the High Alpha organization. Fmr. Founder/CEO of Holder (web3 CRM, 2022-2024). ExactTarget → Salesforce alum. Based in Indianapolis.
pain_themes: Running Claude Code in isolation — wants multi-agent NanoClaw-style setup
Compliance/trust barrier: work accounts can't access confidential email/Google data via LLMs
Data governance in agentic systems — needs control layer, not just capability
Wants to see how to move from tool-user to infrastructure-builder
motivations: Live demo of NanoClaw multi-agent setup
Understand container isolation model as answer to his trust/compliance concern
Arthur & Archie intro — understand what AA does and if there's a fit
Peer conversation: fellow Claude Code daily user
communication_style: Direct, curious, founder-mindset. Self-described startup junkie. Faith-oriented (lists as 'Believer'). Creative background (musician). Frames everything through storytelling and brand.
relationship_notes: Introduced via Joel context. Reached out via LinkedIn/DM. Meeting tomorrow (Monday March 10). Shares Claude Code usage. Compliance concern re: Google data is the primary objection to NanoClaw adoption. Alloy has corporate partners at F500 level (Eli Lilly, Elanco, Huntington) — warm vector into enterprise clients. Holder shutdown 2024 — well-received departure, still respected in ecosystem.

firm_details.name: Alloy Partners
firm_details.model: Venture studio — co-creates startups with F500 corporations
firm_details.focus: B2B SaaS, enterprise software, seed to Series A

background.founder_experience: Built and shut down Holder (web3 CRM) — timing issue, not execution. Returned capital to investors. No regrets framing.

conversation_context.from_joel: Asked if he has a Claw yet. He wants it but: no good machine, doesn't fully trust it yet, work accounts not allowed.
conversation_context.drew_quote: I'm running Claude Code basically all day now.
conversation_context.joel_angle: Are you running Claude Code at root or in isolation? If the former, it's less secure than what many of the Claw designs do. I build mine with Claude Code forking NanoClaw.
conversation_context.drew_response: Yeah mostly in isolation not in root. Biggest thing is more confidential email or calendar or Google data. Let's definitely chat soon! Would love a demo.
conversation_context.key_insight: He's already sold on Claude Code. The question is trust + infrastructure architecture. NanoClaw's container isolation is the direct answer to his concern.

meeting_angles: Show container isolation model — his 'trust' concern is exactly what NanoClaw solves architecturally
Google MCP + isolated container = his Google data stays scoped, doesn't leak to model
He's a daily Claude Code user — speak peer-to-peer, not vendor
Alloy's F500 corporate partners are warm enterprise intro vectors for AA
Holder story = risk tolerance + founder credibility + web3 timing lesson (he understands 'too early')
Music background — shared creative/analytical dual lens with Joel
He's already curious: 'Would love a demo' — this is a warm yes to show, not pitch
